contents.value:
```tsx
export default function Page({ params, searchParams }: PageProps): JSX.Element
```
A `page.tsx` is the unique UI of a route in the App Router. It is the default export that renders when the route is matched. Pages receive `params` for dynamic segments and `searchParams` for query parameters.

```tsx
export default function Layout({ children, params }: LayoutProps): JSX.Element
```
A `layout.tsx` defines shared UI for a segment and its children. Layouts preserve state, remain interactive, and do not re-render on navigation. The root layout must contain `<html>` and `<body>` tags.

```tsx
export default function Loading(): JSX.Element
```
A `loading.tsx` creates instant loading UI with React Suspense. The content is displayed while the segment's content loads. It automatically wraps the page in a `<Suspense>` boundary.

```tsx
'use client'
export default function Error({ error, reset }: { error: Error & { digest?: string }; reset: () => void }): JSX.Element
```
An `error.tsx` defines an error UI boundary for a route segment. It must be a Client Component. Receives `error` (the thrown error) and `reset` (a function to attempt re-rendering).

```tsx
export default function NotFound(): JSX.Element
```
A `not-found.tsx` renders UI when the `notFound()` function is thrown within a route segment. It also serves as a catch-all for unmatched URLs at the app level.

```tsx
export default function Template({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }): JSX.Element
```
A `template.tsx` is similar to `layout.tsx` but creates a new instance on each navigation. It does not preserve state between navigations and re-mounts DOM elements.

```tsx
export async function GET(request: NextRequest, context: { params: Promise<Params> }): Promise<NextResponse>
```
A `route.ts` file defines a Route Handler. Route Handlers allow you to create custom request handlers for a given route using the Web Request and Response APIs.

```tsx
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server'

export function middleware(request: NextRequest): NextResponse
```
Middleware runs before a request is completed. It can rewrite, redirect, modify headers, or respond directly. Define in `middleware.ts` at the project root.

```tsx
export default function Default({ params }: { params: Params }): JSX.Element
```
A `default.tsx` is a fallback for parallel routes when Next.js cannot recover a slot's active state after a full-page load. It serves as the default content for `@slot` folders.

```tsx
'use client'
export default function GlobalError({ error, reset }: { error: Error & { digest?: string }; reset: () => void }): JSX.Element
```
A `global-error.tsx` handles uncaught errors in the root layout. It must define its own `<html>` and `<body>` tags since it replaces the root layout when active.

```tsx
export async function generateStaticParams(): Promise<Array<{ [key: string]: string }>>
```
Used in combination with dynamic route segments to statically generate routes at build time instead of on-demand at request time. Returns an array of params objects for each route to pre-render.

```tsx
export async function generateMetadata({ params, searchParams }: Props, parent: ResolvingMetadata): Promise<Metadata>
```
Generates dynamic metadata for a page. Receives route params and parent metadata. Returns a `Metadata` object that sets `<head>` elements like title, description, Open Graph, etc.

```tsx
import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'

revalidatePath(path: string, type?: 'page' | 'layout'): void
```
Purges cached data for a specific path on-demand. Can be used in Server Actions or Route Handlers. The `type` parameter controls whether to revalidate a page or its layout tree.

```tsx
import { revalidateTag } from 'next/cache'

revalidateTag(tag: string): void
```
Purges cached data associated with a specific cache tag on-demand. Tags are assigned via `fetch` options: `fetch(url, { next: { tags: ['tagName'] } })`. Use in Server Actions or Route Handlers.

```tsx
'use client'
```
The `"use client"` directive marks a component as a Client Component. Client Components are rendered on the client and can use React hooks like `useState`, `useEffect`, event handlers, and browser APIs. Must be at the top of the file.

```tsx
'use server'
```
The `"use server"` directive marks a function or module as a Server Action. Server Actions can be called from Client Components and run exclusively on the server. They can be used in form actions and event handlers.

```tsx
import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation'

const router = useRouter(): AppRouterInstance
```
Returns a router instance for programmatic navigation in Client Components. Methods: `router.push(href)`, `router.replace(href)`, `router.refresh()`, `router.prefetch(href)`, `router.back()`, `router.forward()`.

```tsx
import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation'

const pathname = usePathname(): string
```
Returns the current URL pathname as a string. For example, `/dashboard/settings`. Client Component hook that reads the current route. Updates on navigation.

```tsx
import { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation'

const searchParams = useSearchParams(): ReadonlyURLSearchParams
```
Returns a read-only `URLSearchParams` object for accessing the current URL's query parameters. Must be used in a Client Component and wrapped in a `<Suspense>` boundary.

```tsx
import { useParams } from 'next/navigation'

const params = useParams(): Params
```
Returns an object containing the current route's dynamic parameters. For example, for `/shop/[slug]` visiting `/shop/1` returns `{ slug: '1' }`. Client Component hook.

```tsx
import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'

redirect(path: string, type?: RedirectType): never
```
Redirects the user to another URL. Can be used in Server Components, Server Actions, and Route Handlers. Throws a `NEXT_REDIRECT` error internally. Default type is `'replace'`.

```tsx
import { notFound } from 'next/navigation'

notFound(): never
```
Invokes the `not-found.tsx` file for the current route segment. Throws a `NEXT_NOT_FOUND` error that terminates rendering of the current segment and renders the not-found UI.

```tsx
import { useSelectedLayoutSegment } from 'next/navigation'

const segment = useSelectedLayoutSegment(parallelRoutesKey?: string): string | null
```
Returns the active route segment one level below the layout it's called from. Useful for navigation UI like tabs that change style based on the active child segment.

```tsx
import { useSelectedLayoutSegments } from 'next/navigation'

const segments = useSelectedLayoutSegments(parallelRoutesKey?: string): string[]
```
Returns an array of active route segments below the layout it's called from. Useful for building breadcrumbs or other UI that needs knowledge of all active child segments.

```tsx import Image from 'next/image' ``` The `next/image` component extends HTML `` with automatic image optimization: lazy loading, responsive sizing, format conversion (WebP/AVIF), and prevents layout shift.

```tsx
src: string | StaticImport
```
The source of the image. Can be a path string (local or remote), or a statically imported image file. Remote images require `remotePatterns` configuration in `next.config.js`.

```tsx
width: number
```
The intrinsic width of the image in pixels. Required unless using `fill` prop or a statically imported image. Used to determine the correct aspect ratio and avoid layout shift.

```tsx
height: number
```
The intrinsic height of the image in pixels. Required unless using `fill` prop or a statically imported image. Used to determine the correct aspect ratio and avoid layout shift.

```tsx
alt: string
```
Descriptive text for the image used by screen readers and search engines. Required prop on the `next/image` component for accessibility compliance.

```tsx
fill?: boolean
```
Causes the image to fill its parent element instead of using explicit width/height. The parent must have `position: relative`. Use with `sizes` prop and `object-fit` CSS.

```tsx
sizes?: string
```
A media query string that indicates how wide the image will be at various breakpoints. Important for performance when using `fill` or responsive images. Example: `"(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 50vw"`.

```tsx
quality?: number // 1-100, default 75
```
The quality of the optimized image. Higher values produce better quality but larger file sizes. Integer between 1 and 100.

```tsx
priority?: boolean // default false
```
When true, the image is considered high priority and will be preloaded. Disables lazy loading. Use for the Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) element on each page.

```tsx
placeholder?: 'empty' | 'blur' | 'data:image/...' // default 'empty'
```
A placeholder to show while the image is loading. `'blur'` uses `blurDataURL` (auto-generated for static imports). `'empty'` shows nothing during load.

```tsx
blurDataURL?: string
```
A base64-encoded Data URL used as the placeholder image before `src` loads. Only takes effect when combined with `placeholder="blur"`. Auto-generated for statically imported images.

```tsx
import Link from 'next/link'

<Link href={string | UrlObject} prefetch={boolean} replace={boolean} scroll={boolean}>{children}</Link>
```
The `next/link` component enables client-side navigation between routes with automatic prefetching. It extends HTML `<a>` with Next.js routing capabilities.

```tsx
href: string | UrlObject
```
The path or URL to navigate to. Can be a string path (`"/dashboard"`), an object with `pathname` and `query`, or a full URL for external links.

```tsx
prefetch?: boolean | null // default: true
```
Controls prefetching behavior for `next/link`. When `true`, the linked route is prefetched when the link enters the viewport. Set to `false` to disable prefetching.

```tsx
replace?: boolean // default false
```
When `true`, `next/link` replaces the current history state instead of adding a new entry to the browser's history stack.

```tsx
scroll?: boolean // default true
```
Controls whether the page scrolls to the top after navigation. Set to `false` to maintain the current scroll position when navigating via `next/link`.

```tsx
import localFont from 'next/font/local'

const myFont = localFont({ src: './my-font.woff2', display?: string, variable?: string })
```
Loads a local font file. Supports `.woff`, `.woff2`, `.ttf`, `.otf`. Automatically self-hosts and optimizes fonts with zero layout shift.

```tsx
import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'

const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'], display?: string, variable?: string, weight?: string | string[] })
```
Loads Google Fonts with automatic self-hosting. No requests are sent to Google by the browser. Supports variable and non-variable fonts.

```tsx
import Script from 'next/script'

<Script src={string} strategy={string} onLoad={Function} onReady={Function} onError={Function} />
```
The `next/script` component enables loading third-party scripts with optimized strategies: `beforeInteractive`, `afterInteractive` (default), `lazyOnload`, and `worker`.

```tsx
strategy?: 'beforeInteractive' | 'afterInteractive' | 'lazyOnload' | 'worker'
```
Controls when the third-party script loads. `beforeInteractive`: before hydration. `afterInteractive` (default): after hydration. `lazyOnload`: during idle time. `worker`: in a web worker.

```tsx
onLoad?: () => void
```
Callback executed once after the script has finished loading. Only works with `afterInteractive` and `lazyOnload` strategies.

```tsx
onReady?: () => void
```
Callback executed after the script has finished loading and every time the component is mounted. Useful for scripts that need re-initialization on route changes.

```tsx
import { headers } from 'next/headers'

const headersList = await headers(): Promise<ReadonlyHeaders>
```
Returns a read-only Headers object for the incoming request. Used in Server Components and Route Handlers. Dynamic function that opts into dynamic rendering.

```tsx
import { cookies } from 'next/headers'

const cookieStore = await cookies(): Promise<ReadonlyRequestCookies>
```
Returns a read-only cookies object. Methods: `.get(name)`, `.getAll()`, `.has(name)`. To set cookies, use `cookies().set()` in a Server Action or Route Handler.

```tsx
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'

class NextResponse extends Response {
  static json(body, init?): NextResponse
  static redirect(url, status?): NextResponse
  static rewrite(destination): NextResponse
  static next(init?): NextResponse
  cookies: ResponseCookies
}
```
Extends the Web `Response` API with helper methods for Next.js middleware and route handlers.

```tsx
type NextMiddleware = (
  request: NextRequest,
  event: NextFetchEvent
) => NextResponse | Response | null | undefined | void | Promise<...>
```
The function type for Next.js middleware. Receives the request and an event object with `waitUntil()` for extending the lifetime of the function.

```tsx
export const revalidate = number | false | 0
```
Sets the default revalidation time (in seconds) for a layout or page segment. `0` means always revalidate. `false` means cache indefinitely. Applies to all `fetch` requests in the segment.

```tsx
next: { tags: string[] }
```
Assigns cache tags to a `fetch` request. Tags enable on-demand revalidation via `revalidateTag(tag)`. Example: `fetch(url, { next: { tags: ['posts'] } })`.

```tsx
cache?: 'force-cache' | 'no-store'
```
The `cache` option on `fetch()` controls request caching. `'force-cache'` (default for GET in Server Components) caches the response. `'no-store'` skips the cache entirely.

```tsx
import { unstable_noStore as noStore } from 'next/cache'

noStore(): void
```
Opts a Server Component or function out of static rendering, ensuring it always runs dynamically. Equivalent to `cache: 'no-store'` on a fetch request.

```tsx
import type { Metadata } from 'next'

export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: string | { default, template, absolute },
  description: string,
  openGraph: { ... },
  twitter: { ... },
  icons: { ... },
  robots: { ... }
}
```
Static metadata export for a page or layout. Generates `<head>` elements including title, description, Open Graph tags, Twitter cards, icons, and robots directives.

```tsx
import type { Viewport } from 'next'

export const viewport: Viewport = {
  width: 'device-width',
  initialScale: 1,
  maximumScale: 1,
  themeColor: string | { media: string, color: string }[]
}
```
Static viewport configuration export. Separated from metadata for better static optimization. Controls viewport meta tag and theme color.

```tsx
openGraph: {
  title: string,
  description: string,
  url: string,
  siteName: string,
  images: [{ url, width, height, alt }],
  locale: string,
  type: 'website' | 'article'
}
```
Open Graph metadata for social media sharing. Generates `og:` meta tags. Used within the `metadata` or `generateMetadata` exports.

```tsx
twitter: {
  card: 'summary' | 'summary_large_image' | 'app' | 'player',
  title: string,
  description: string,
  images: string[],
  creator: string
}
```
Twitter Card metadata. Generates `twitter:` meta tags for rich link previews on Twitter/X.

```tsx
icons: {
  icon: string | { url, type, sizes }[],
  shortcut: string,
  apple: string | { url, sizes }[],
  other: { rel, url }[]
}
```
Favicon and app icon metadata. Generates `<link rel="icon">`, `<link rel="apple-touch-icon">`, and other icon-related tags.

```tsx import dynamic from 'next/dynamic' const Component = dynamic(() => import('./Component'), { loading: () =>

Loading...

, ssr: boolean }) ``` Lazy loads a component with code splitting. Supports SSR toggle and a loading component. Uses `React.lazy()` and `Suspense` under the hood.

```tsx
import { useFormStatus } from 'react-dom'

const { pending, data, method, action } = useFormStatus()
```
Returns status information for the last form submission. `pending` is `true` while the Server Action is executing. Must be used inside a `<form>` element.

```tsx
import { useFormState } from 'react-dom'

const [state, formAction] = useFormState(action: ServerAction, initialState: State)
```
Manages form state with a Server Action. Returns the current state and a wrapped action. The action receives previous state and form data as arguments.

```tsx
import { useOptimistic } from 'react'

const [optimisticState, addOptimistic] = useOptimistic(state, updateFn)
```
Provides optimistic UI updates. Shows a temporary state while an async action (like a Server Action) is in progress. Reverts to actual state when the action completes.

```tsx
export async function GET(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: Promise<Params> }): Promise<Response | NextResponse>
```
HTTP GET handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Handles GET requests. Cached by default unless using dynamic functions.

```tsx
export async function POST(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: Promise<Params> }): Promise<Response | NextResponse>
```
HTTP POST handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Handles POST requests. Not cached by default.

```tsx
export async function PUT(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: Promise<Params> }): Promise<Response | NextResponse>
```
HTTP PUT handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Handles PUT requests for full resource updates.

```tsx
export async function PATCH(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: Promise<Params> }): Promise<Response | NextResponse>
```
HTTP PATCH handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Handles PATCH requests for partial resource updates.

```tsx
export async function DELETE(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: Promise<Params> }): Promise<Response | NextResponse>
```
HTTP DELETE handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Handles DELETE requests for resource removal.

```tsx
export async function HEAD(request: NextRequest): Promise<Response>
```
HTTP HEAD handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Similar to GET but without a response body. Useful for checking resource existence or headers.

```tsx
export async function OPTIONS(request: NextRequest): Promise<Response>
```
HTTP OPTIONS handler in a Route Handler (`route.ts`). Used for CORS preflight requests. If not defined, Next.js auto-handles OPTIONS based on other defined methods.

```
app/
  @analytics/
    page.tsx
  @team/
    page.tsx
  layout.tsx
  page.tsx
```
Parallel routes are defined by naming a folder with the `@` prefix (slot). Each slot is passed as a prop to the parent layout. Parallel routes render simultaneously in the same layout.

```
(.) - matches the same level
(..) - matches one level above
(..)(..) - matches two levels above
(...) - matches from the root app directory
```
Intercepting routes convention. Allows loading a route within the current layout while showing a different URL. Commonly used for modals (e.g., photo gallery overlays).

```
(..) - Intercepts a route one level above the current segment.
```
Used for intercepting routes one directory level above. Place inside a folder like `(..)photo/[id]/page.tsx` to intercept `/photo/[id]` from a sibling route.

```
(...) - Intercepts a route from the root app directory.
```
Used for intercepting routes from the root of the app directory structure. Matches regardless of nesting depth.

```
(..)(..) - Intercepts a route two levels above the current segment.
```
Used for intercepting routes two directory levels above. Useful for deeply nested interception patterns.

```tsx
export const runtime = 'nodejs' | 'edge'
```
Sets the runtime for a route segment. `'nodejs'` (default) uses the Node.js runtime. `'edge'` uses the Edge Runtime with a smaller API surface but faster cold starts.

```tsx
export const dynamicParams = true | false
```
Controls behavior when visiting a dynamic segment not generated with `generateStaticParams`. `true` (default) generates on demand. `false` returns a 404.

```tsx
export const fetchCache = 'auto' | 'default-cache' | 'only-cache' | 'force-cache' | 'default-no-store' | 'only-no-store' | 'force-no-store'
```
Overrides the default caching behavior of all `fetch` requests in a route segment. Advanced option for fine-grained cache control.

```tsx
import type { NextPage } from 'next'

const Page: NextPage<Props> = ({ prop1, prop2 }) => { ... }
```
Type for page components in the Pages Router. Generic type that accepts page props. Includes `getInitialProps` support.

```tsx
import type { NextApiRequest } from 'next'

interface NextApiRequest extends IncomingMessage {
  query: Partial<{ [key: string]: string | string[] }>
  cookies: Partial<{ [key: string]: string }>
  body: any
  env: Env
}
```
Type for API route request objects in the Pages Router (`pages/api/`). Extends Node.js `IncomingMessage` with parsed query, cookies, and body.

```tsx
import type { NextApiResponse } from 'next'

interface NextApiResponse<T = any> extends ServerResponse {
  status(code: number): NextApiResponse<T>
  json(body: T): void
  redirect(url: string): NextApiResponse<T>
  send(body: string): void
}
```
Type for API route response objects in the Pages Router. Provides helper methods for status codes, JSON, redirects, and sending responses.

```tsx
shallow?: boolean
```
When used with `next/link` or `router.push`, updates the URL without running data fetching methods (Pages Router). In the App Router, use `window.history.pushState` instead.

```nextjs
function permanentRedirect(url: string, type?: RedirectType): never
```
Redirects the user with a 308 permanent redirect status. Can be used in Server Components, Route Handlers, and Server Actions.

```nextjs
static NextResponse.json(body: any, init?: ResponseInit): NextResponse
```
Creates a JSON response with the given body and optional ResponseInit options including status, headers.

```nextjs
static NextResponse.redirect(url: string | URL, status?: number): NextResponse
```
Creates a redirect response to the specified URL with an optional status code (defaults to 307).

```nextjs
static NextResponse.rewrite(url: string | URL, init?: MiddlewareResponseInit): NextResponse
```
Rewrites the request to a different URL while preserving the original URL in the browser.

```nextjs
static NextResponse.next(init?: MiddlewareResponseInit): NextResponse
```
Continues the middleware chain. Can optionally modify request headers for downstream processing.

```nextjs
function generateViewport(props: { params: Params; searchParams: SearchParams }): Viewport | Promise<Viewport>
```
Generates dynamic viewport configuration for a page. Returns viewport-related meta tags.

```nextjs
function Font(options: FontOptions): NextFont
```
Font optimization with automatic self-hosting, zero layout shift, and built-in CSS variable support. Supports Google Fonts and local fonts.

```nextjs
class ImageResponse extends Response
```
Generates dynamic Open Graph images using JSX and CSS. Supports Edge runtime and Satori rendering engine.

```nextjs
function useRouter(): NextRouter
```
Pages Router hook returning router object with pathname, query, asPath, locale, push, replace, prefetch, back, events.

```nextjs
type Metadata = { title?: string | { absolute?: string; default?: string; template?: string }; description?: string; ... }
```
Type for static and dynamic metadata configuration. Supports title, description, openGraph, twitter, icons, robots, and more.

```nextjs
type Viewport = { themeColor?: string | ThemeColorDescriptor[]; width?: string | number; initialScale?: number; ... }
```
Type for viewport configuration exported from pages and layouts.

```nextjs
type NextConfig = { reactStrictMode?: boolean; images?: ImagesConfig; rewrites?: () => Promise<Rewrite[]>; ... }
```
Type for next.config.js/ts configuration object. Controls build, runtime, and feature settings.

```nextjs type GetServerSideProps

= (context: GetServerSidePropsContext) => Promise> ``` Type for the getServerSideProps function in Pages Router.

```nextjs type GetStaticProps

= (context: GetStaticPropsContext) => Promise> ``` Type for the getStaticProps function in Pages Router.

```nextjs type GetStaticPaths

= () => Promise<{ paths: Array; fallback: boolean | 'blocking' }> ``` Type for the getStaticPaths function in Pages Router.

```nextjs
interface ImageProps extends Omit<ImgHTMLAttributes<HTMLImageElement>, 'src' | 'srcSet' | 'ref' | 'width' | 'height' | ...>
```
Props for the Image component including src, alt, width, height, fill, sizes, quality, priority, placeholder, blurDataURL, loader.

```nextjs
interface LinkProps { href: string | URL; as?: string | URL; replace?: boolean; scroll?: boolean; shallow?: boolean; prefetch?: boolean; locale?: string | false; }
```
Props for the Link component including href, replace, scroll, shallow, prefetch, and locale.

```nextjs
interface ScriptProps { src?: string; strategy?: 'beforeInteractive' | 'afterInteractive' | 'lazyOnload' | 'worker'; onLoad?: () => void; onReady?: () => void; onError?: () => void; }
```
Props for the Script component with loading strategy control and lifecycle callbacks.

```nextjs
type NextFont = { className: string; style: { fontFamily: string; fontWeight?: number; fontStyle?: string }; variable: string; }
```
Return type of next/font font loaders. Provides className and CSS variable for applying the font.

```nextjs
type MiddlewareConfig = { matcher: string | string[] }
```
Configuration object exported from middleware.ts to define which routes the middleware applies to.

```nextjs
type PageProps = { params: Record<string, string>; searchParams: Record<string, string | string[] | undefined> }
```
Props received by App Router page components including dynamic route params and URL search parameters.

```nextjs
type LayoutProps = { children: React.ReactNode; params: Record<string, string> }
```
Props received by App Router layout components including children and dynamic route params.

```nextjs
type Sitemap = Array<{ url: string; lastModified?: string | Date; changeFrequency?: ChangeFrequency; priority?: number; alternates?: { languages?: Record<string, string> } }>
```
Return type for sitemap.ts file that generates XML sitemaps.

```nextjs
type Robots = { rules: RobotsRule | RobotsRule[]; sitemap?: string | string[]; host?: string }
```
Return type for robots.ts file that generates robots.txt.
